localContext: Manhattanville's mix of pre-war buildings, Columbia University properties, and residential developments near Morningside Park creates ideal entry points for stink bugs seeking warmth during colder months. The neighborhood's tree-lined streets and proximity to Riverside Park means seasonal stink bug activity is particularly common in buildings along Broadway and along the hillside areas near St. Mary's Park.

faqs.question: Why are stink bugs so common in Manhattanville apartments?
faqs.answer: Manhattanville's older buildings often have small cracks and gaps around windows, doors, and foundations that stink bugs exploit when seeking winter shelter. The neighborhood's elevation changes and proximity to parks also means higher stink bug populations looking to move indoors during fall. Our treatment seals entry points and eliminates existing populations.

faqs.question: What does your stink bug control service include for $350 – $550?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection of your Manhattanville property, targeted treatment of affected areas, exclusion work to seal entry points, exterior perimeter treatment, and follow-up monitoring. Pricing depends on property size and infestation severity, with most Manhattanville homes falling in the $350 – $550 range.

faqs.question: When is stink bug season in Manhattanville?
faqs.answer: Stink bugs are most active in Manhattanville during fall (September through November) when they seek indoor shelter, and again in spring (March through May) when they emerge from overwintering sites. We recommend preventative treatments in early fall before the invasion begins and early spring to prevent re-entry.

faqs.question: Are your stink bug treatments safe for Columbia University area housing?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved treatments that are safe for residential environments, including family homes and student housing near Columbia. Our technicians are experienced working in Manhattanville's diverse properties and always prioritize the safety of residents, pets, and the environment while effectively eliminating stink bug problems.
